description: Frontmatter declares `name: mmx-cli` but the directory is `minimax-multimodal-toolkit`; validate_skills.py hard check requires name to match directory name
suggested_fix: Change `name: mmx-cli` to `name: minimax-multimodal-toolkit` in the frontmatter, or rename the directory to `mmx-cli`

description: Downloads `https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh` to /tmp/, chmod +x, then executes it — functionally equivalent to curl-pipe-sh; trusts external URL at runtime
suggested_fix: Pin to a known-good version hash or vendor the installer inside the repo; add SHA256 verification before execution

description: Uses sudo with apt-get, dnf, pacman, and zypper to install system packages; grants root privileges to the install script
suggested_fix: Document sudo requirement explicitly in README; consider offering a no-sudo path via user-local installs or container-based setup

description: Appends `export PATH=$HOME/.dotnet:$PATH` to ~/.bashrc, permanently modifying the user's shell PATH across sessions
suggested_fix: Inform the user of the PATH change before writing; provide an opt-out flag such as --no-profile

description: Runs `pip install reportlab pypdf matplotlib` at runtime without version pins or hash verification; susceptible to supply-chain compromise on dependency update
suggested_fix: Pin all packages to exact versions in a requirements.txt and use `pip install -r requirements.txt --require-hashes`

description: Launches Playwright/Chromium to render HTML to PDF; headless browsers are a significant attack surface and should be scoped to a sandboxed environment
suggested_fix: Run Playwright in a sandboxed container or document that render_cover.js should only process trusted HTML inputs generated by palette.py/cover.py

description: `requests>=2.28` uses a floor constraint with no upper bound; a future major version with breaking changes or vulnerabilities could be installed silently
suggested_fix: Pin to `requests==2.32.3` (or current stable) and add a `# requirements.txt` header noting it should be installed in a virtualenv

description: Agent does not declare a model tier; callers cannot determine expected cost or capability tier
suggested_fix: Add `model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` or `model: claude-sonnet-4-6` to frontmatter based on intended use

description: Agent has zero example invocations; users have no concrete reference for how to call it
suggested_fix: Add at least one `## Examples` block showing a sample invocation with required inputs

description: Agent does not declare a model tier
suggested_fix: Add model declaration to frontmatter

description: Agent has zero example invocations
suggested_fix: Add at least one Examples block

description: Agent does not declare a model tier
suggested_fix: Add model declaration to frontmatter

description: Agent has zero example invocations
suggested_fix: Add at least one Examples block

suggested_fix: Add model declaration to frontmatter

suggested_fix: Add at least one Examples block

description: Agent does not declare a model tier
suggested_fix: Add model declaration to frontmatter

description: Agent has zero example invocations
suggested_fix: Add at least one Examples block

description: Skill does not declare a `license` field; other skills in the same plugin subtree include license
suggested_fix: Add `license: MIT` or appropriate license to frontmatter

description: Skill does not declare a `license` field
suggested_fix: Add `license: MIT` or appropriate license to frontmatter

description: Skill is missing both `license` and `metadata` fields; all peer skills in the repository include both
suggested_fix: Add `license: MIT` and a `metadata` block with `version` and `category`

description: Uses vague quantifiers 'most appropriate layout' and 'optimal layout' without concrete selection criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with specific decision criteria, e.g. 'Use asymmetric layout when content includes an image asset; use centered layout for inspirational/event presentations'

description: Uses 'appropriate layout' and 'effective professional layouts' — 'effective' and 'appropriate' are vague without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit mapping: 'Corporate tone → left-aligned accent; Creative tone → full-bleed; Minimal tone → bold center'

description: Uses 'best subtype' and 'clean, well-structured code' — vague without defining what 'best' means or what structure is required
suggested_fix: Replace 'best subtype' with 'subtype determined by content taxonomy table above'; remove 'clean' — the code standards section already defines the structural requirements

description: Skill name `mmx-cli` differs from directory name `minimax-multimodal-toolkit`; breaks convention enforced by validate_skills.py and causes skill discovery failures
suggested_fix: Align name and directory: either rename the file's `name` field to `minimax-multimodal-toolkit` or rename the directory to `mmx-cli`

description: skills/pptx-generator and plugins/pptx-plugin cover identical PowerPoint generation use cases; both trigger on 'PPT/PPTX/PowerPoint/slide'; users loading both may get ambiguous routing
suggested_fix: Add a note to pptx-generator description clarifying it wraps pptx-plugin workflow and should be preferred for standalone installations without the plugin
